LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR VIOLATION OF SAN FRANCISCO ADMINISTRATIVE CODE 6.22(J) IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT AN AMENDED PROPOSED ORDER THAT INCLUDES LANGUAGE THAT 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND WAS GRANTED BY THE COURT. THE PROPOSED ORDER IS TO BE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2019 Continued To Mar-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Aaron Gruber, Esq. 650-445-0543 of Ralls Gruber & Niece for Plaintiff.
Alexandra Drury, Esq. 415-397-2823 of Seyfath Shaw for Defendant TJPA.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues CCP 1061. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief in the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Although not raised in the demurrer, the sixth cause of action is likely invalid due to the failure of plaintiff Skanska USA Civil West California District, Inc. to file a government claim against TJPA because the sixth cause of action, fairly construed, seeks release of retained funds. (See Canova v. Trustees of Imperial Irrigation District Employee Pension Plan (2007) 150 Cal. App. 4th 1487, 1493 (government claim required for a mandamus claim where the primary purpose of the claim is recovery of money or damages)). In all events, because Skanska has adequate remedies against Webcor, the declaratory relief claim is neither necessary nor proper at this time per CCP 1061.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Continued To Jan-23-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2018 Continued To Dec-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aaron Gruber, Esq. of Ralls Gruber & Niece, 650.445.0543, appearing for Plaintiff Skanska;
Michael T. McKeeman, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, 415.544.1038, appearing for Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's.

Ruling: DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is ARGUED.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as modified: Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's demurrer to the sixth cause of action in the amended complaint for violation of San Francisco Administrative Code 6.22(j) is sustained WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND to allege the declaratory relief claim. Government Code 905 requires that all claims for money or damages against a public entity be presented to that public entity prior to filing a lawsuit. Pleading compliance with the section 905 or an excuse for noncompliance, which applies to both contract and tort claims, is an element of plaintiff Skanska USA Civil West California District, Inc.'s section 6.22(j) claim. (State v. Superior Crt. (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1240 ("We conclude that failure to allege facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the claim presentation requirement subjects a claim against a public entity to a demurrer for failure to state a cause of action"); City of Stockton v. Superior Crt. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 734 ("we hold that these requirements apply to breach of contract claims.")). Skanska seeks "money damages" from TJPA, which is a public entity. The prevailing party is to submit an amended proposed order that includes language that 20 days leave to amend was granted by the Court. The proposed order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
